trade.summary: Brazil exports soybeans, iron ore, corn, beef, and crude oil to Japan. Japan exports vehicles, machinery, electronics, and chemicals to Brazil. Japan is one of Brazil's top export destinations in Asia.

diplomatic.summary: Brazil and Japan have exceptionally warm ties rooted in the massive Japanese immigration to Brazil — Brazil hosts the largest Japanese diaspora outside Japan (~1.5 million). Regular bilateral summits, cultural exchanges, and strong people-to-people ties.

military.summary: No military alliance. Japan's pacifist constitution limits military cooperation. Some naval contact and interest in Brazilian defense procurement. Japan has supplied surveillance aircraft to Brazil.

financial.summary: Japan is a significant investor in Brazil, particularly in agriculture, automotive, and manufacturing. JBIC and JICA have financed major Brazilian infrastructure projects. Japanese companies including Toyota, Honda, and Mitsubishi have major operations in Brazil.

energy.summary: Japan imports Brazilian crude oil and ethanol. JBIC has funded Brazilian energy projects. Cooperation on offshore oil technology. Japan interested in Brazilian hydrogen exports.

scientific.summary: Strong scientific cooperation. JICA supports agricultural and environmental research in Brazil. Joint programs in agronomy (PRODECER legacy), biodiversity, and materials science. Large Nikkei-Brazilian academic community bridges both countries.
